narrative: You notice a low creeping plant forming a dense carpet along the shaded woodland edge. You crush a leaf between your fingers...
interesting_fact: Ground Ivy was the main flavouring in British ale before hops were introduced in the 16th century — its brewing name Alehoof comes from this use.

questions.question: What plant is this?
What is the common name of this plant?
Does Ground Ivy have any other common names?
options: It has no other common names

questions.question: What is the botanical name of Ground Ivy?
Where is Ground Ivy typically found?
options: Woodland edges, hedgerows and shaded moist ground
Rocky coastal paths and cliff tops
Dry chalky grassland in full sun
questions.correct: Woodland edges, hedgerows and shaded moist ground

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Ground Ivy
options: Kidney shaped leaves with rounded toothed edges on square stems
Strong minty and aromatic smell when the leaves are crushed
Tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing
Heart shaped leaves in groups of three
Milky white sap when the stem is broken
correct: Kidney shaped leaves with rounded toothed edges on square stems
Strong minty and aromatic smell when the leaves are crushed

questions.question: What is Ground Ivy used for? Select all that apply
options: Young leaves added to salads or soups
Made into a tea or infusion
Historically used to flavour beer before hops
Eaten raw in large quantities as a staple food
Used as a rope or cordage material
correct: Young leaves added to salads or soups
Made into a tea or infusion
Historically used to flavour beer before hops

questions.question: What does Ground Ivy taste like?
options: Earthy and slightly bitter with minty undertones
questions.correct: Earthy and slightly bitter with minty undertones

questions.question: What are the risks or cautions with Ground Ivy? Select all that apply
options: Avoid during pregnancy — contains similar compounds to mint which can stimulate contractions
Avoid with kidney or liver problems
Best in spring — older leaves become tough and bitter
Toxic to horses and cattle in large quantities
Toxic to humans even in small amounts
correct: Avoid during pregnancy — contains similar compounds to mint which can stimulate contractions
Avoid with kidney or liver problems
Best in spring — older leaves become tough and bitter
Toxic to horses and cattle in large quantities

questions.question: Which species could Ground Ivy be confused with?
When does Ground Ivy flower?
options: Midsummer — small white star shaped flowers

questions.question: What time of year is Ground Ivy most commonly found?
Is Ground Ivy native to the UK?
options: It was introduced by Roman settlers

questions.question: What was Ground Ivy historically used to treat? Select all that apply
How was Ground Ivy applied medicinally?
options: Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
Applied as a compress soaked in cold water

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Ground Ivy?
options: Biennial — takes two years to complete
Ephemeral — only lasts a few weeks

questions.question: What soil or habitat conditions does Ground Ivy prefer?
options: Moist, shaded ground — tolerates a range of soils
Dry sandy soil in full sun
questions.correct: Moist, shaded ground — tolerates a range of soils

questions.question: What plant family does Ground Ivy belong to?
What is the origin of the name Glechoma?
options: From Greek meaning a type of mint
Named after the botanist Heinrich Glechom
questions.correct: From Greek meaning a type of mint

questions.question: What does Hederacea mean in the botanical name?
options: Ivy-like — referring to its creeping habit
Bitter tasting — referring to its flavour
Shade loving — referring to its habitat
Milk producing — referring to its sap
questions.correct: Ivy-like — referring to its creeping habit
